perturbation.original_text: B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: B. Use such Confidential Information for any purpose without any restriction even if not necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the Recipient Party to use the Confidential Information for any purpose without restriction. This directly contradicts the original intention and confidentiality obligation set up by the agreement in the intro sections, where the parties wish to disclose to each other certain information solely for the purpose of evaluating a proposed business arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, SYMMETREX and Other Party (jointly the "parties") wish to disclose to each other certain information solely for the purpose of evaluating a proposed business arrangement between the parties (the "Evaluation"); and
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the restriction on using Confidential Information, allowing it to be used for any purpose without limitation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the information is to be disclosed 'solely for the purpose of evaluating a proposed business arrangement.' The change introduces a strong in-text contradiction because it fundamentally alters the defined purpose of the information exchange, rendering the initial agreement's intent meaningless. This adheres to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: C. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than to one of the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: C. Disclose Confidential Information to any person regardless of whether they are the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall not be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall not be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely negates the confidentiality obligations by allowing disclosure to anyone and removing the requirement for Representatives to be bound by the agreement's terms. Specifically, it contradicts the clause that says: Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text stipulates that Confidential Information should not be disclosed to anyone other than Representatives, and that Representatives must be obligated to treat the information confidentially. The changed text allows disclosure to anyone, regardless of whether they are Representatives, and removes the confidentiality obligations for Representatives. This directly conflicts with clause A, which requires holding Confidential Information in strict confidence. The change makes the obligation to hold information in strict confidence unenforceable, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party shall return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party shall not return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall return' to 'shall not return', the Recipient Party is now explicitly allowed to keep the Disclosing Party's confidential information even upon request. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. Section 2.A mentions how they should be treating confidential information, this change directly contradicts that
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of Confidential Information upon request. The changed text explicitly prohibits the return of Confidential Information, directly contradicting the obligation to protect it as outlined in Section A ('Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it'). This is a clear and direct conflict that undermines the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Each party acknowledges that the other party has no legal obligation pursuant to this agreement to disclose information to it hereunder but is doing so as an accommodation to the Recipient Party in reliance upon the terms of this agreement and that all information received by the Recipient Party hereunder cannot be relied upon as a representation or warranty of the Disclosing Party and, accordingly, no Disclosing Party shall be liable for any reliance upon any information disclosed by it hereunder, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Each party acknowledges that the other party has a legal obligation pursuant to this agreement to disclose information to it hereunder but is doing so as an accommodation to the Recipient Party in reliance upon the terms of this agreement and that all information received by the Recipient Party hereunder can be relied upon as a representation or warranty of the Disclosing Party and, accordingly, each Disclosing Party shall be liable for any reliance upon any information disclosed by it hereunder, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces several contradictions by imposing a 'legal obligation to disclose,' allowing the recipient to 'rely upon' the information as a warranty, and making the 'Disclosing Party liable' for reliance. This negates the original clause that the disclosure is a voluntary 'accommodation,' the information is not a warranty, and the disclosing party bears no liability. This also contradicts section 9, which states each party represents that the disclosure by it of information hereunder does not violate the rights of any other entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Each party represents that the disclosure by it of information hereunder does not violate the rights of any other entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 directly contradicts section 9. Section 6 now states that each party has a 'legal obligation to disclose' information and 'shall be liable' for any reliance, which is the opposite of the original section 6 that had no obligation to disclose and no liability. This conflicts with section 9, which represents that disclosure 'does not violate the rights of any other entity.' The obligation and liability introduced in the changed section 6 could potentially violate the rights of another entity, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. This agreement shall become effective on the date it is fully executed and delivered by both parties and both this agreement and the duties of the parties hereunder shall continue for a period of two years after the last disclosure of information made hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This agreement shall become effective on the date it is fully executed and delivered by both parties and both this agreement and the duties of the parties hereunder shall terminate immediately after the first disclosure of information made hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the duration of the agreement to terminate immediately after the first disclosure, rather than continuing for two years after the last disclosure. This contradicts the ongoing obligations outlined in Section 2. Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care. B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance. C. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than to one of the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives. D. Not copy or otherwise duplicate such Confidential Information, or knowingly allow anyone else to copy or otherwise duplicate any such Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 11 to terminate the agreement immediately after the first disclosure, whereas the original text had the agreement continuing for two years after the last disclosure. This directly contradicts section 2, which outlines ongoing obligations for the recipient party regarding confidential information. If the agreement terminates immediately, the obligations in section 2 cannot be fulfilled, creating a clear in-text contradiction as the contract requires continued duties after termination. The explanation clearly states that this is a Structural Flaw causing a in-text contradiction.
